findings.message: New `device_class` dimension is read from `params`, but the ingest route filters params through the fail-closed allowlist (`frontend_rum_allowed_query_params`, routes/frontend_rum.py:64), which contains only the `utm_*` keys. `device_class` is never in the allowlist, so it is stripped before reaching `record_page_view` and the `app_frontend_device_class_total` series can only ever emit `device_class="unknown"`. The signal is dead-on-arrival: it produces no operable data.
findings.suggestion: Add `device_class` to `frontend_rum_allowed_query_params` in src/demo/config/settings.py (so the backend allowlist passes it through), or source it server-side (e.g. derived from User-Agent) rather than from the allowlisted query params. Add a unit test asserting a non-`unknown` device_class series renders end-to-end through the route.

findings.message: New RUM signal `app_frontend_device_class_total` is exposed on /metrics with no corresponding Grafana dashboard panel (infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/frontend.json has panels only for web vitals, js_errors, page_views, rum_beacons) and no alert rule (infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/frontend_alerts.yml covers only LCP and js_errors). The metric is emitted but unobserved by any operator-facing surface.
findings.suggestion: Add a panel to frontend.json (e.g. `sum(rate(app_frontend_device_class_total[5m])) by (device_class)`) and, if device-class breakdown is meant to gate anything, a corresponding alert rule. The repo's obs-completeness guard expects scrape+dashboard parity for declared signals.
